reasoning: The stock closed at roughly 300 (specifically 299.97 on 2026-01-30). The historical baseline expectation is that stocks slowly drift upward in the short to medium term. For the first resolution date (Feb 8, 2026), the stock has only one week of trading. P(higher) should remain very close to the base rate (~51-52%), but analyst downward revisions and high valuations (P/E ~ 35x) may exert slight downward pressure. However, recent (Jan 2026) buy recommendations have been initiated, counteracting this negative sentiment. For the second date (March 3, 2026), there will be a Q4 2025 earnings report on Feb 17. The earnings report increases the variance of the outcome, adding a large dose of volatility. This typically makes the probability shift towards 0.5 marginally, but the longer time horizon inherently increases likelihood of a positive drift due to general market positive drift. Base rate for a 1-month climb is closer to 53-54%, but given the soft guidance reports from late 2024 and earnings nervousness, a slight haircut to 52% is appropriate. Both dates are thus very close to a coinflip, reflecting typical market drift with no exceedingly strong directional catalyst prior to the outcomes.
